events.title: US Conducts Further Strikes Against Iran Amid Escalating Tensions
events.desc: The U.S. military announced on Tuesday that it was launching additional strikes against Iran. This action follows a period of heightened conflict and precedes the U.S. reinstatement of a naval blockade of Iranian shipping. The strikes began Tuesday afternoon Eastern Time, according to U.S. Central Command.

events.title: Iran-linked Vessels Transit Hormuz Ahead of US Blockade
events.desc: The number of vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz increased on Tuesday, with most linked to Iranian trade, just before a U.S. naval blockade took effect. President Donald Trump had reimposed the blockade on all Iranian ports and threatened further action against infrastructure if negotiations did not resume.

events.title: US and Iran Exchange Fire as Tensions Intensify Over Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The United States and Iran engaged in a tit-for-tat exchange of strikes after incidents involving tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. military reported hitting missile sites and command centers, while Iran claimed to have attacked U.S. bases in response.

events.title: US Insists Strait of Hormuz Remains Open Amid Exchange of Strikes with Iran
events.desc: Despite Iran's earlier claims of closing the Strait of Hormuz and launching attacks on U.S. allies and regional bases, the U.S. military maintained that the vital waterway remains open. This statement comes amidst ongoing retaliatory strikes.

events.title: Trump Threatens More Strikes on Iran, Tehran Warns of 'Fearless' Response
events.desc: President Trump indicated that the U.S. would likely conduct further strikes on Iran, stating that any escalation would be brief. This threat comes as Tehran issued a warning of a 'fearless' response to any further aggression.

events.title: US Military Launches More Strikes Against Iran
events.desc: U.S. Central Command confirmed on Tuesday afternoon Eastern Time that it was conducting further strikes against Iran. These actions are part of an ongoing escalation involving naval blockades and retaliatory attacks in the region.

events.title: Israeli Strike Kills Seven in North Gaza Police Post
events.desc: An Israeli military strike in northern Gaza killed seven individuals, including a senior officer in the Hamas-run police force. The Israeli military stated that the targets were "terrorists."

events.title: Houthi Missiles Target Saudi Arabia Following Strikes on Sanaa Airport
events.desc: The Iran-backed Houthi group launched missiles targeting Saudi Arabia, specifically Abha airport, in retaliation for an attack on Sanaa airport in Yemen. The Houthis blamed Saudi Arabia for the strike in Yemen's capital.

events.title: US Lawmakers Introduce Bill to Counter Transnational Repression by China and Iran
events.desc: A bipartisan bill was introduced in the U.S. Congress to enhance penalties for foreign government agents threatening individuals on U.S. soil. This initiative aims to address "transnational repression" tactics employed by countries like China and Iran.

events.title: Trump Welcomes Iraqi PM to White House, Vows Deeper Economic Ties
events.desc: U.S. President Donald Trump met with Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi at the White House, pledging to strengthen economic relations between the two countries. The meeting also addressed the planned withdrawal of U.S. troops from Iraq by September 30.

events.title: UK Bans Support for Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps
events.desc: The United Kingdom has officially banned support for Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC). The government cited the group's links to death threats and intimidation on British soil as the reason for the designation.

events.title: UN Rights Chief: Return to US-Iran Hostilities is Setback for Civilians
events.desc: The U.N. High Commissioner for Human Rights, Volker Turk, stated that the resumption of hostilities between the United States and Iran is a severe blow to civilians in the region. He warned of significant disruptions to essential supplies and increased regional instability.

events.title: Iran Claims Damage on US Gulf Bases After U.S. Strikes
events.desc: Following U.S. strikes, Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claimed to have inflicted damage on U.S. military bases in the Persian Gulf. President Trump indicated that strikes on Iran would continue.

events.title: UAE Condemns Iran's 'Brazen' Tanker Attack Amid Fresh US Strikes
events.desc: The United Arab Emirates condemned Iran's actions concerning tankers in the Strait of Hormuz as 'brazen.' This condemnation follows a third consecutive night of U.S. strikes against Iran, with President Trump announcing a new blockade and a 20% charge on ships transiting the strait.

events.title: US Military Continues Strikes Against Iran
events.desc: The U.S. military confirmed on Tuesday it was carrying out further strikes against Iran, escalating the ongoing conflict. These actions come as the U.S. prepares to reinstate a blockade on Iranian shipping after Tehran indicated it had closed the Strait of Hormuz.
